{{Plamo_Infobox|image = 1-100-Gundam-Barbatos-6th-Form-box.jpg|Classification = 1/100 IRON-BLOODED ORPHANS|Lineup no. = 05|Scale = 1/100|Franchise = Mobile Suit Gundam IRON-BLOODED ORPHANS|Release Date = March 19, 2016|Price = ¥3,240 {{refyentax}}|JAN/ISBN = 4549660073239|Need glue? = No|Materials = PS,PE|Run = Standard|Japanese Name=1/100 ガンダムバルバトス 第6形態|Model of=ASW-G-08 Gundam Barbatos}}The [[1/100 IRON-BLOODED ORPHANS]] (NG IBO) '''ASW-G-08 Gundam Barbatos''' is a 1/100 scale kit released in 2016.

== Includes==

*Gundam Barbatos
**2 gray backpack arm pieces (extended)
**5th Form Parts
***x2 Barbatos Shoulder Armors
***Reactive Armor
***Waist Booster joint
***x2 Barbatos' waist armors
***2x2 Mortars
****x2 Autocannon panels for Ground Type
**6th Form parts
***Chest Armor
***x2 Graze Ritter Shoulder Armors
***x2 Boosters
**Manipulators
***Weapon-holding (1 pair)
***Open-palm (1 pair)
*Sub-arms for Backpack
**1 Long
**1 Short 
*Large Special Mace
*Mace
**Pile Bunker
*x2 Adapter for Maces

==Kit Features & Gimmicks==

=== Articulation ===

*The head can swivel on a ball-and-socket neck joint.
*The torso can tilt forwards, or sideways (with functioning pistons).
*The arms can...
**Swivel on a ball-and-socket joint.
**Raise horizontally.
*The upper arms can rotate.
*Elbow joints can be bent up to 120 degrees.
*The manipulators can swivel on a ball-and-socket joint.
*The waist can rotate 360 degrees.
* The hip joint axis can swing frontward/backwards on one side.
* The upper thigh joints can swing frontwards (and backwards in some extent) on a peg-and-socket joint, raise horizontally, and/or rotate on each piece.
**Front and side skirt armors can lift up to provide clearance.
*The knees are double-jointed.
*Ankle joints can pivot and/or slightly tilt.
*The Ground Type Waist Boosters are jointed, allowing some articulation.

=== Weapons/Other Gimmicks ===
*Armor can be attached or removed from the Gundam Frame. (Some parts required swapping.)
*The hands are swappable.
*Any of the weapons can be wielded by the default weapon-holding hands.
**The weapon handles/hilts and the palms feature molded tabs and tab holes for increased gripping stability.
* Gundam Barbatos can be built into:
** '''5th Form''': Reactive Armor + Arm Mortars + 4th Form Shoulder Armors + [[1/100 EB-05s Schwalbe Graze (McGillis Fareed Custom)|1/100 McGillis' Schwalbe Graze]]'s (or [[1/100 EB-05s Schwalbe Graze (Gaelio Baldwin Custom)|Gaelio's]]) Booster Panels & Waist Booster Joint
** '''5th Form (Ground Type)''': Autocannons + 4th Form Shoulder Armors + Regular Barbatos Waist Armors
** '''6th Form''': Autocannons + additional Chest Armor + Graze Ritter Shoulder Armors + Ground Type Waist Boosters
*Backpack arms can be attached to the sub-arms (long and short).  
**The Backpack Arms' grey parts can be swapped with the alternate pieces.
* Large Special Mace can...
** Be rearranged into attack mode.
** Attach onto either of the the backpack sub-arms via an adapter.
* The Mace...
** Features a removable pile bunker.
** Can attach onto either of the the backpack sub-arms via an adapter.
* The rear end for the Ground Type boosters can be opened or closed.
* The 6th form Shoulder Armors can also attach onto the [[1/100 EB-06 Graze|1/100 Graze]]'s or [[1/100 EB-05s Schwalbe Graze (McGillis Fareed Custom)|1/100 McGillis' Schwalbe Graze's]] (Both sold separately) shoulder armor joints. 
* Gundam Barbatos can mount onto [[Action Base]]<nowiki/>s 1 or 4 via an adapter.
**If one is inclined, the adapter's panels can be shortened by trimming the pieces off. (Note: may be unstable if trimmed too much.)

==Tips and Tricks ==
* For better results, some or most of the details are needed to be applied with Gundam Markers or Mr. Color paints.
* Topcoat (Mr. Hobby recommended) can be used to protect applied stickers and if any, marking decals and painted details.

=== Customizing-based tips ===

* If one wants a more accurate space-use 5th Form, the legs must be swapped with the [[1/100 ASW-G-08 Gundam Barbatos|1/100 Gundam Barbatos]]' regular feet parts.
** Additionally, the 6th Form's kit includes a pair of surplus Barbatos feet parts for builders to use; however, swapping these parts requires disassembly and a replacement of the polycap joint.

==Variants==
:{{CurrentReissues}}

==Notes & Trivia==

* While the Gunpla comes with most of the surplus pieces of Barbatos' 4th form, it's not possible to create a complete kit as it lacks the blue elbow parts.
